summary: Verified iter14 contact-fix + new q/date_from/date_to filters. Backend: 6/6 pytest passed — (1) live 254,LLC contact_id coverage on Plaid rows is now well above the 90% bar (post-backfill), (2) q='starbucks' returns 41 rows all containing 'starbucks' in merchant/description/contact, (3) regex specials 'AT&T', '$5.00', '(*', '[abc]', '.*', '\d+' all return 200 (re.escape works), (4) date_from/date_to bounds honored, (5) q + needs_review + pagination composes and pagination.total is stable across pages with disjoint row ids, (6) unit test with monkeypatched pipeline confirms contact_resolver.resolve_contacts_batch is called ONCE with ALL 3 candidates (both PFC-primary + LLM-deferred) and every inserted doc carries non-null contact_id/contact_name. Frontend: on 254,LLC Transactions page, initial 'Showing 1–250 of 1,871'; typing 'starbucks' → 'Showing 1–41 of 41' (debounced ~300ms); X-clear resets both input and results; date range 2024-06-01..2024-06-30 filters correctly; 'Clear filters' button appears only when filters active and resets search+dates; regex-special 'AT&T' returns 47 rows without error, '$5.00' returns 0 without error.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py list_transactions: date range clause uses lexicographic string comparison on ISO dates — fine as long as ALL rows store dates as 'YYYY-MM-DD'. Consider adding a stored index on {company_id: 1, date: -1} if not already present; count_documents + find + skip on 1871+ rows can become slow as the corpus grows.

plaid_connect.categorize_and_insert_plaid_txns: contact_resolver.resolve_contacts_batch is invoked once with ALL candidates before per-item DB inserts — good. However when a Plaid txn has empty merchant_name the fast path is still skipped but the AI fallback path is bounded by semaphore=5 which is reasonable.

Transactions.jsx has two useEffects that both depend on debouncedSearch/dateFrom/dateTo — the reset-to-page-1 effect fires on the same trigger as the load effect. Race is benign because setPage no-ops when already 1, but consolidating page-reset into the setter (setSearch/setDateFrom callers) would eliminate a duplicate GET whenever the user paginates then edits a filter.

Search input has no minimum length: a single-character query hits the API 300ms after each keystroke. Consider gating fetch until debouncedSearch.length===0 or >=2 to reduce load on very large corpora.
seed_data_creation: Unit test creates a throwaway company id 'test-<uuid>' with 4 seeded accounts, then tears everything down in finally.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Note: navigating directly to /transactions before switching company redirects to /dashboard. To land on 254,LLC Transactions: (1) login, (2) header company-switcher → click get_by_text('254, LLC', exact=True).first, (3) then click sidebar 'Transactions' link. Path lands at /accounting/transactions. Search debounce is ~300ms; wait 1.2s+ after fill() before asserting on indicator. 'Clear filters' button only rendered when filtersActive is true.
